title: Amplitude API keys should not be disclosed
description: Amplitude is a product analytics platform used to track user behavior, funnels, retention, and revenue. Amplitude credentials include API keys (for data ingestion) and secret keys (for the management API). Exposing these credentials allows unauthorized access to analytics data, user event streams, and the ability to send fraudulent analytics events.
recommendation: Remove Amplitude credentials from source code. Store API keys and secret keys in environment variables or a secrets manager. Rotate exposed keys immediately in Amplitude's Settings > Projects.
impact: Unauthorized access to user analytics data, behavioral event streams, funnel data, revenue metrics, and the ability to corrupt analytics data by injecting fake events.

checks.message: Amplitude API key detected in variable assignment. Amplitude API keys are used for data ingestion via the HTTP API, SDK initialization, and event tracking. Exposing this key allows attackers to send fraudulent events corrupting your analytics, inflate user metrics, inject false funnel data, and access real-time event streams. This can completely corrupt your analytics data making business decisions unreliable. Rotate immediately in Amplitude Settings > Projects.

Amplitude Secret Key detected in variable assignment. Amplitude secret keys authenticate management API requests for accessing user data, creating charts, managing experiments, and exporting raw event data. Exposing this key allows unauthorized access to all user behavioral data, A/B test results, cohort definitions, and raw event exports containing personally identifiable information. Rotate immediately in Amplitude Settings.

Amplitude credential detected near Amplitude keyword. A 32-character hex string associated with Amplitude may be an API key or secret key used for analytics event tracking or management API access. Verify and rotate if confirmed as a hardcoded credential.
